sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, this really comes down to sugar control.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 11.8g of carbs. fibre is where things start to fall off. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 0.9g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, the cleanest wins are low sugar and decent fibre.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: vitamin C reputation is a real strength because that gives it a clear in-category edge. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: protein contribution is tiny because that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth. sugar can climb fast because a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Orange has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
